description: A speaker 'nosheku' an earlier claim or proposal when they withdraw it from what they now put forward. The earlier words remain part of the record; retraction changes the speaker's present commitment to them. 'lilea' (clarify) makes meaning plainer, while correction identifies or replaces an error.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the sound through the nose, and round the lips into middle-height back 'o'. Raise the tongue blade towards the postalveolar region for unvoiced 'sh' and release into stressed middle-height front 'e'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and finish with high-back rounded 'u'.
usage_notes: The withdrawn claim or proposal is the object. Ending consent, permission, or a continuing commitment uses the construction appropriate to that relation rather than treating every ending as retraction.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A speaker can withdraw an untenable claim without pretending it was never spoken. Revision does not erase the record or reduce the speaker's standing.

semantic_domains.cognition: a changed commitment to what one previously put forward
semantic_domains.communication: withdrawal of an earlier claim or proposal from one's present contribution
